change_summary: Decider patched detail_evidence DOI 10.1007/978-3-662-03137-4_11, removed HeartMath 404 link (replaced with Sentman 1987 via doi.org/10.1029/RS022i004p00595), and corrected Schumann 1952/1954 wording (theoretical prediction 1952, first measurement König & Schumann 1954). EXP-197 (pole-floor argument) and EXP-198 (defense) pending analyst — NOT yet landed, so pole-floor reframing, finding/tldr wording, and anchor imprecision persist. Residual gap: a SECOND broken Nickolaenko DOI 10.1007/978-3-662-03137-5 still in detail_verdict_text — decider's DOI patch missed this sibling occurrence.
our_argument_summary: WIN-038 argues the dome's claim of a stable 7.83 Hz Schumann resonance (a) is a post-hoc textbook fact (first measured 1954, continuously monitored since the 1960s), not a genuine prospective prediction under our Part 3.1b test, and (b) self-contradicts the dome's own cavity geometry, which via the quarter-wave formula f = c/(4H) — the same formula WIN-029 back-derives from — predicts 14-42 Hz under various averagings of H(r) = 8,537 × exp(-r/8,619) km, and 8.78 Hz (the minimum) at the pole. Verdict: Self-Contradicted.

relevant_files: raw-text/05-model.txt — H(r) formula and Schumann back-derivation H = c/(4×7.83) = 9,572 km
monitor.py / pull_data.py — not present in raw-text/ for this cycle; findings carried forward from c1/c2 code inspection
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Hardcoded identity check: predicted 7.83 Hz, observed 7.83 Hz (or 7.5-7.83 Hz Tomsk-referenced). No live Schumann data fetched. No temporal validation of 'persistence'. Dome's model page back-derives H from known 7.83 Hz, not the reverse.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Entirely hardcoded. Static pred==obs identity. No API call.
data_sources_used: Tomsk Space Observation System (referenced in PROS-008 text, not fetched by automation)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: ±0.3 Hz tolerance window (7.5-8.13 Hz) absorbs diurnal drift and places falsification nearly out of reach absent a G4+ storm.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new this cycle. monitor.py was not in raw-text/ so no fresh inspection was possible; tags are carried forward with the same caveats as c3.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Shared Schumann domain with WIN-029; prior cycles confirmed static pred=7.83, obs=7.83 with no fetch. monitor.py absent from raw-text/ this cycle, so no fresh inspection — tag carried forward unchanged from c3.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Schumann stability is textbook ionospheric physics (Sentman 1987; Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002). The fundamental is globe-circumference-derived; decadal variation ≤ ±0.1 Hz. The dome relabels 'ionospheric cavity' as 'conductive firmament cavity' and claims credit for the same stability without deriving 7.83 Hz from dome geometry.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Schumann theoretical prediction 1952; König & Schumann first measurement 1954; continuous monitoring since the 1960s. Dome registered PROS-008 on 2026-03-06 — 74 years after the theoretical paper and 72 years after first measurement. A 6-day stability window on a 74-year-stable phenomenon has essentially zero falsification probability.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No forward derivation. The dome's H(r) = 8,537 × exp(-r/8,619) km applied forward to f = c/(4H) gives 8.78 Hz at the pole (MINIMUM for the radial profile), and 22-35 Hz under standard disc-averaging — none of which is 7.83 Hz. The model page performs only a BACK-derivation H = c/(4×7.83) = 9,572 km, which additionally exceeds its own H(r=0) = 8,537 km ceiling.

holes_found.description: Residual broken DOI in detail_verdict_text: https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-662-03137-5 (link text 'Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002') still returns 404 (verified this cycle via WebFetch). The c3 review flagged this EXACT URL, and the decider's patch claimed 'DOI 404' was resolved — but only the sibling DOI in detail_evidence (10.1007/978-3-662-03137-4_11) was actually replaced; detail_verdict_text was missed. A defender who clicks the Nickolaenko link in the Verdict section still sees '404 Not Found' and the citation-fabrication narrative survives. This is a regression-of-patch, not a new fault — the decider's commit should have fixed both occurrences.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the broken DOI in detail_verdict_text with the same Springer URL already used in detail_evidence: Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002. Single-line patch. Also add a build-time check (or a curmudgeon holistic lens) that greps all HTML-bearing text fields for the broken Springer DOI prefix 10.1007/978-3-662-03137 to prevent this class of sibling-occurrence misses.

holes_found.description: tldr_evidence wording 'Standard ionospheric physics explains 7.83 Hz stability from the ~100 km ionospheric height on a spherical Earth' invites a dome-defender gotcha. The Schumann fundamental frequency is set by Earth's CIRCUMFERENCE (~40,075 km) via the globe-resonance formula f ≈ c√(n(n+1))/(2πR), not by the ionospheric ceiling height. If a dome defender applies quarter-wave to 100 km they get 749 Hz — 100× off — and can say 'even your own TLDR doesn't understand Schumann physics.' The ionospheric height governs cavity LOSSES and damping (why observed 7.83 is below the idealized lossless 10.6 Hz), not the fundamental frequency.
holes_found.recommendation: Rephrase to: 'Standard ionospheric physics explains 7.83 Hz via the globe's circumference (~40,075 km), with the ionospheric D-layer providing the upper conductive boundary and finite-conductivity damping that lowers the fundamental from an idealized 10.6 Hz (lossless) to the observed ~7.83 Hz.' This is accurate and closes the gotcha. The tldr_verdict already avoids this trap by saying 'globe ionospheric physics' without a height figure — keep that as-is.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD from c3 (tracked in EXP-197, not yet landed): WIN-038's finding ('Dome's own cavity predicts ~22 Hz'), tldr_evidence ('baseline Schumann frequency of ~22 Hz'), and tldr_verdict ('predicts ~22 Hz, not 7.83 Hz') all lead with the simple-average framing, which is the WEAKEST form of the self-contradiction. The pole-floor framing — 'the dome's H(r) maxes at 8,537 km at r=0, giving f_min = 8.78 Hz; 7.83 Hz is below the dome's own geometric floor at every radius' — is strictly stronger (no averaging choice required, quantitatively verified this cycle: f(H=8537) = 299792/(4×8537) = 8.779 Hz). The c3 review recommended this rewrite; EXP-197 has been created to service it. Not a new finding, but still unpatched — flagging so the decider doesn't treat c3's recommendation as closed.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action — decider already queued EXP-197 for analyst. This cycle's note is a status marker only: c3 issue remains open pending analyst prose. If EXP-197 has not landed within ~48h, consider whether a manual patch (finding, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict updated to pole-floor wording) is warranted ahead of the full expansion.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD from c3: detail_extra field still duplicates the OTS/timestamp argument that detail_verdict_text now handles at length, so a reader who expands both sees the argument twice. Low impact (they are different UI blocks — collapsible note vs. verdict text), but reader attention is finite.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: trim detail_extra to a one-liner pointer such as 'Timestamp issue — see Verdict below and Timestamp Error tab.' Not blocking.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD from c3: detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text cross-reference 'Section 2.1' and 'Part 3.1b' via anchors #part2 and #posthoc-standard respectively. The #part2 anchor lands the reader at the top of Part 2 rather than at the 2.1 Schumann subsection (no unique id on the 2.1 <details> wrapper). On a long page this is friction. The #posthoc-standard anchor works correctly.
holes_found.recommendation: Add id='p2-21' (or similar) to the Section 2.1 <details> element in sections.json, then update the href in detail_evidence from '#part2' to '#p2-21'. One-line fix each side. Low priority.

stronger_arguments.description: Triple-value self-contradiction on H: the dome's own documents give THREE mutually incompatible values for the relevant cavity height. (i) H(r=0) = 8,537 km from the exponential formula — implies f_min = 8.78 Hz at the pole. (ii) firmament_height = 9,086 km from the model's stated parameters (used in WIN-029's 'match') — implies f = 8.25 Hz (5.3% above observed). (iii) Back-derived H = c/(4×7.83) = 9,572 km — exceeds BOTH declared values and has no place to go in the dome's geometry (it's above the stated firmament and above the H(r) maximum). No single dome number produces 7.83 Hz; the dome can't point to any internally consistent H that matches observation. WIN-029 already captures the 8,537 vs 9,086 discrepancy; WIN-038 should co-opt the 9,572 third leg to make the triple-bind explicit.
sources: data/wins.json WIN-029 detail_evidence (H₀=8,537 vs firmament_height=9,086, 6.4% discrepancy)
raw-text/05-model.txt line 64 (dome's own H = c/(4×7.83) = 9,572 km)
Quantitative check this cycle: f(8537)=8.78 Hz, f(9086)=8.25 Hz, f(9572)=7.83 Hz

stronger_arguments.description: Cross-WIN double-counting argument: WIN-002, WIN-029, and WIN-038 all describe the same single observation (a stable ~7.83 Hz Schumann fundamental). The dome claims three independent WINs from one measurement by splitting it into 'cavity exists' / 'needs conductive ceiling' / 'frequency stable' — which is a form of methodological inflation. Section 2.1 alludes to this; WIN-038 should reference it explicitly so a reader of the WIN panel understands they are reading one-third of a triple-counted claim.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome is correct that the Schumann fundamental IS remarkably stable on decadal timescales (±0.1 Hz) — this stability is genuinely a robust feature of the Earth-ionosphere system. It is also correct that ANY model of global ELF resonance must explain both WHY the frequency sits near 7.83 Hz AND why it stays there; stability-by-itself is a non-trivial constraint that rules out many toy models. The dome author is not wrong to treat this as something a cosmological model should be able to account for.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Standard ionospheric physics handles BOTH requirements with no free parameters: the circumference-derived mode sets ~10.6 Hz, ionospheric losses drop it to ~7.83 Hz, and the ionospheric profile's own stability (driven by solar flux regularity and known diurnal/seasonal patterns) provides the decadal stability. The dome model, by contrast, (a) cannot derive 7.83 Hz from any internally consistent height value, and (b) does not supply an independent mechanism for why the wrong frequency it does predict would nonetheless stay constant. A model that gets both 'value' and 'stability' wrong does not earn credit for 'stability alone'.

advocate_mode.best_defense: The quarter-wave formula f = c/(4H) is a vertical standing-wave approximation for a parallel-plate cavity — it does not govern the actual Schumann mode, which is a TRANSVERSE (TM) resonance around the cavity's horizontal dimension. Applied to a globe: f ≈ c × sqrt(n(n+1)) / (2π R_earth) ≈ 10.6 Hz idealized for n=1, dropping to ~7.83 Hz under finite-conductivity corrections. For the dome, the analog is a transverse mode around the disc's horizontal extent — disc diameter ~40,030 km, remarkably close to Earth's 40,075 km circumference. The curmudgeon's forward-calc attacks a mode the dome does not physically use. The dome's back-derivation via H = c/(4×7.83) is a one-time numerical exercise to quote a height figure, not a claim that quarter-wave IS the mode formula.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Our text must preempt this by: (1) pointing out that 05-model.txt line 64 EXPLICITLY uses f = c/(4H) — so the dome is on record using the quarter-wave relation, and cannot now disown it; (2) if the dome retreats to a transverse-diameter mode, the 40,030 km ≈ 40,075 km match is a Recurring-Pattern-#4 number-copy from globe geometry without independent derivation; (3) even under transverse-mode interpretation, the dome would owe an explanation for why its radial H(r) profile — which differs from a uniform sphere — does not perturb the frequency away from the globe's 7.83 Hz. The dome cannot keep the globe's frequency while claiming different geometry.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Our WIN-002 / WIN-029 / WIN-038 reviews remain internally consistent — all three treat Schumann resonance as a single observation triple-counted by the dome. The INTERNAL contradiction lives on the dome side: its 8,537 / 9,086 / 9,572 km heights are mutually incompatible (see stronger_arguments). This cycle adds: the c3 recommended pole-floor reframe (EXP-197) has not landed yet, so WIN-038 still LEADS with a weaker averaging argument while Section 2.1 already uses the stronger pole-floor framing — a mild stronger-text-in-aggregate-than-in-detail inversion. Not a contradiction, but a coverage hole that EXP-197 addresses.

claims_checked: Section 2.1: 'minimum 8.77 Hz at the pole' (c3 cited this value).
WIN-029: 'H₀ = 9,086 km used to derive 8.26 Hz, matching 7.83 Hz to 5.3%'.
WIN-038 detail_evidence: 'simple radial average ~3,400 km gives ~22 Hz'.
WIN-038 finding: 'Dome's own cavity predicts ~22 Hz'.
quantitative_verification.computations: c = 299,792 km/s. f(H=8537) = 299792/(4×8537) = 8.779 Hz → confirms Section 2.1's 8.77 Hz (rounding). f(H=9086) = 299792/(4×9086) = 8.249 Hz → confirms WIN-029's 5.3% match (|8.249 - 7.83|/7.83 = 5.3%). H(7.83) = 299792/(4×7.83) = 9,571.9 km → confirms WIN-029's 9,572 km back-derivation. Simple radial average of H(r) = 8537×exp(-r/8619) over r ∈ [0, 20015]: (1/R)∫₀ᴿ H(r) dr = 8537×(8619/20015)×(1 - exp(-20015/8619)) = 3,316 km → f = 22.6 Hz → confirms '~3,400 km' and '~22 Hz' to within rounding. Area-weighted average (2/R²)∫₀ᴿ r·H(r) dr = 2,135 km → f = 35.1 Hz — slightly higher than the text's '~1,800 km / ~42 Hz'; both fall within the stated 14-42 Hz range and the argument holds, but the specific 1,800 km figure is closer to 2,135 km under the standard 2D-area weighting.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Area-weighted H: text '~1,800 km', recomputation 2,135 km. Uses a different weighting convention (e.g., uniform-linear-r slices vs. true 2D disc element). Both round into the stated 14-42 Hz range, so the argument is preserved; no material error, but the specific 1,800 km could tighten to 2,100 km for higher precision. Not a hole — flagging for eventual tidy-up when EXP-197 lands the pole-floor reframe and may replace the averaging discussion anyway.

deeper_analysis:
This cycle is narrow by design: the decider's parallel patch landed three of four c3 fixes cleanly (detail_evidence DOI, HeartMath, 1952/1954 wording) but missed a sibling occurrence of the same broken DOI in detail_verdict_text. That is the only new critical finding. The pole-floor reframing, finding/tldr wording update, and anchor precision are all known-pending in EXP-197 / EXP-198 and should not be raised as fresh holes.

Two structural observations worth surfacing to the tinker, not as holes:
(a) The 'broken DOI in sibling field' failure mode is likely a class, not an instance — the decider fixed the occurrence in detail_evidence without grep-checking detail_verdict_text, detail_extra, and tldr_* for the same URL substring. A build-time or curmudgeon-holistic grep-for-broken-DOI-prefixes pass would catch this class before it reaches the site. Worth a tinker proposal.
(b) The c3-to-c4 delta demonstrates the value of 'patch landed' checks: the queue reason claimed 'DOI 404 patched' but only one of two DOIs was actually replaced. A decider pop-time check that the review's cited failing URLs no longer appear in any text field would close the gap.

On the substance: the WIN's verdict (Self-Contradicted) holds. Nothing in the decider's patches weakens it; if anything, replacing HeartMath with Sentman 1987 strengthens the textbook-Schumann framing. Once EXP-197 and EXP-198 land, WIN-038 will shift from 'well-argued but not leading with the strongest framing' to 'leading with the pole-floor minimum, with averaging as supporting'. No risk to the verdict.

citations_verified: Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002 'Resonances in the Earth-Ionosphere Cavity' (Kluwer/Springer, ISBN 978-1-4020-0754-5) — the book is real; the Springer URL https://link.springer.com/book/9781402007545 resolves correctly (cited in detail_evidence).

Sentman 1987 'Magnetic elliptical polarization of Schumann resonances', Radio Science 22, 595-606 — DOI https://doi.org/10.1029/RS022i004p00595 redirects to the AGU/Wiley landing page (agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1029/RS022i004p00595); DOI resolves, underlying paper is real (cited in detail_verdict_text — the replacement for HeartMath).
Schumann (1952) theoretical prediction and König & Schumann (1954) first measurement — historical references, not DOI'd in our text; well-established in ionospheric physics literature.

The decider's DOI patch landed on the detail_evidence occurrence but missed this sibling occurrence in detail_verdict_text. This is the single critical hole this cycle (see holes_found).
